messages.content:
The Eiffel Tower is a wrought-iron lattice tower located on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France. It is named after the engineer Gustave Eiffel, whose company designed and built the structure. The tower was constructed between 1887 and 1889 as the entrance arch to the 1889 World's Fair, marking the 100th anniversary of the French Revolution. Standing at 324 meters (1,063 feet) tall, the Eiffel Tower was the tallest man-made structure in the world until the Chrysler Building in New York City was completed in 1930.

The Eiffel Tower is an iconic symbol of France and one of the most recognizable structures in the world. It attracts millions of visitors annually, making it one of the most popular tourist attractions globally. The tower features three levels for visitors, with restaurants on the first and second levels, and an observation deck on the third level offering panoramic views of Paris. Its unique design and historical significance make it an enduring architectural landmark.
